senses.definition: plural of reagency; unspecified or uncommon term relating to agencies that are reactivated or reorganized in a particular context.
examples: The project explored the historical reagencies in the regional administration.
Researchers studied the reagencies involved in the emergency response system.

senses.definition: a term indicating the act or process of forming or organizing anew an agency; rarely used in modern English.
examples: The government proposed a reagency of the public health services.
A reagency of the nonprofit sector was discussed at the conference.

senses.definition: a substance used in a chemical reaction to cause or test a chemical change.
examples: Add the reagent to the mixture to identify the compound.
The chemist prepared the reagent for the experiment.

senses.definition: plural of reagent; substances used in chemical reactions.
examples: The lab stocked several reagents for organic synthesis.
Different reagents were tested to optimize the assay.

senses.definition: to aggravate again; to make a situation or condition worse once more.
examples: The delay could reaggravate the health crisis.
Repeating the errors would reaggravate existing problems.

senses.definition: the act of aggravating again; a renewed worsening.
examples: There was a reaggravation of the storm damage after the aftershock.
The patient faced reaggravation of symptoms despite treatment.

senses.definition: to gather or form into a new or different aggregate or group.
examples: They planned to reagglomerate the data to reveal new patterns.
The committee decided to reagglomerate subcommittees for the project.

senses.definition: describing something that has been formed into a new aggregate again.
examples: The reaggregated data set showed new insights.
Reaggregated groups were evaluated for coherence.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of reaggregate; forms into a new aggregate again.
examples: The teams reaggregates their findings each quarter.
They reaggregates the participants to balance the sample.

senses.definition: present participle of reaggregate; in the process of forming into a new aggregate again.
examples: The researchers are reaggregating the data to remove biases.
They are reaggregating the teams to optimize collaboration.

senses.definition: the act or process of forming into a new aggregate again.
examples: The reaggregation of data helped clarify the overall trend.
Methodologies for reaggregation were discussed in class.

senses.definition: plural of reaggregation; multiple instances of forming into new aggregates.
examples: The study compared several reaggregations of the dataset.
Different reaggregations produced varying interpretations.

senses.definition: expressing or demonstrating aggression again or more strongly.
examples: The dog showed reaggressive behavior after the update to the enclosure.
The policy was described as reaggressive in the debate.

senses.definition: a type of antibody (the reagin group) involved in certain immune responses; historically used in allergy testing.
examples: Historically, reagin antibodies were studied in relation to hypersensitivity.
The term reagin appears in older immunology texts.

senses.definition: relating to reagins; of or denoting reagin antibodies.
examples: Reaginic tests were used in early allergy research.
The reaginic response varied among individuals.

senses.definition: in a manner related to reagins or reaginic reactions.
examples: The assay behaved reaginarily rather reagainically in the test.
They discussed reagininically how allergies developed.
